pndf_enriched.name: PARACETAMOL
pndf_enriched.contraindications: Severe hepatic impairment or severe active liver disease.
pndf_enriched.precautions: WARNING: Massive overdose may cause hepatic necrosis and, less frequently, renal damage. Less overdoses can frequently cause reversible jaundice. Has been associated with cases of acute liver failure, at times resulting in liver transplant and death. Most are associated with the doses that exceed the maximum daily limits, and often involve more than 1 paracetamol-containing product. Sodium restriction (soluble paracetamol products may contain large amounts of sodium); Phenylketonuria (soluble products of paracetamol may contain aspartame); Alcohol dependence; overdosage; G6PD deficiency; Hepatic impairment and renal impairment; (dose-related toxicity; Avoid large doses, e.g., >4 g daily); Pregnancy (not known to be harmful); Lactation (small amount is present in milk; short courses are safe in usual dosage; monitor infant).
pndf_enriched.adverse_reactions: [Oral, Rectal] Common and Less Common: Skin rash, nephrotoxicity (chronic overdose), anemia, hyperuricemia, leukopenia, neutropenia, renal disease, pancytopenia Rare: Hypersensitivity reaction [Inj.] Common: Hypertension, hypotension, peripheral edema, tachycardia, headache, insomnia, agitation, anxiety, fatigue, trismus, pruritus, skin rash, hypervolemia, hypoalbuminemia, hypokalemia, hypophosphatemia, nausea, vomiting, hypomagnesemia, constipation, abdominal pain, diarrhea, oliguria, anemia, infusion site reaction, limb pain, muscle spasm, periorbital edema, atelectasis, abnormal breath sounds, dyspnea, hypoxia, pleural effusion, pulmonary edema, stridor, wheezing, fever Less Common: Anaphylaxis, hepatic injury (dose-related), hypersensitivity reaction, severe dermatological reaction (acute generalized exanthematous pustulosis, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis)
pndf_enriched.drug_interactions: Monitor closely with: Enhances therapeutic effect of Vitamin K Antagonists, e.g., Warfarin (anticoagulant effect) Increases metabolism of Paracetamol: Barbiturates e.g. Phenobarbital; Carbamazepine Increases risk of adverse or toxic effects of Paracetamol: Barbiturates; Carbamazepine; Ethyl alcohol (liver damage); Isoniazid Increases risk of adverse or toxic effects of the following drugs: Dapsone, Topical; Fosphenytoin-Phenytoin; Imatinib; Sorafenib (hepatotoxic effect); Nitric Oxide, Prilocaine, Sodium nitrite (significant methemoglobinemia) Reduces therapeutic effect of Paracetamol: Barbiturates, Carbamazepine Avoid concomitant use with: Decreases absorption of Paracetamol: Cholestyramine Resin [administer 1 hour apart to minimize this effect] Increases risk of adverse or toxic effects of Dasatinib (hepatotoxic effect) Increases serum concentration of Paracetamol: Dasatinib; Probenecid (increases the potential for formation of the toxic NAPQI metabolite); Sorafenib TEST INTERACTION.
pndf_enriched.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 and has no significant antiinflammatory activity and is particularly useful for treating mild-to-moderate pain, for reducing fever, and in patients where salicylates and other NSAIDs are contraindicated. Pregnancy & Remarks
pndf_enriched.dosage_instructions: Mild-to-moderate pain, pyrexia, by mouth, ADULT, 0.5–1 g every 4–6 hours (maximum, 4 g daily); CHILD 6–12 years, 250–500 mg; CHILD 1–5 years, 120–250 mg; CHILD 3 months to 1 year, 60–125 mg or 10–15 mg/kg per dose; may repeat doses in children every 4–6 hours if necessary (maximum, 4 doses in 24 hours); by rectum, ADULT, ADOLESCENT, and CHILD =12 years, 650 mg every 4–6 hours (maximum daily dose, 3,900 mg); CHILD >6 up to 12 years, 325 mg every 4–6 hours (maximum daily dose, 1,625 mg); CHILD >3 to 6 years, 120 mg every 4–6 hours (maximum daily dose, 600 mg); CHILD and INFANT 12–36 months, 80 mg every 4–6 hours (maximum daily dose, 400 mg); INFANT 6–11 months, 80 mg every 6 hours (maximum daily dose, 320 mg daily); by IV injection, ADULT =50 kg, 650 mg every 4 hours or 1,000 mg every 6 hours (maximum single dose, 1,000 mg per dose; maximum daily dose, 4 g daily); ADULT <50 kg, 15 mg/kg every 6 hours or 12.5 mg/kg every 4 hours (maximum single dose, 15 mg/kg per dose or 750 mg/dose; maxi
pndf_enriched.administration: For oral administration, may be taken with or without food. Taking with food may decrease GI upset. When administering suspensions, shake well before use. For rectal administration, remove the wrapper and insert the suppository well up into the rectum. For IV administration, administer by IV infusion only. Administer undiluted over 15 minutes. Use within 6 hours of opening vial or transferring to another container. For doses <1,000 mg (<50 kg), withdraw the appropriate dose from the vial and place in a separate empty, sterile container prior to administration. Small volume pediatric doses (up to 600 mg [60 mL]) may be placed in a syringe and infused over 15 minutes via syringe pump. For 1,000 mg doses (=50 kg), insert a vented IV set through vial stopper. Dose Adjustment Renal Impairment: Longer dosing intervals and reduce total doses. Avoid prolonged or repeated use. Hepatic Impairment: Use with caution and avoid prolonged or repeated use in any type of liver disease. Contraindicated
pndf_enriched.pregnancy_category: C Visitors: 35494 Searches: 175821 Department of Health Philippines © 2026 Home Statistics Updates FAQ How to Use FAQs How to Use
